testing.title: Testing Guide
testing.description: Universal Sync Service uses a multi-layered testing strategy to ensure reliability across core logic, host integration, and browser management console.
testing.levels.title: Test Levels
testing.levels.description: We categorize tests into three levels based on their scope and dependencies.
testing.levels.unit.name: Unit Tests
testing.levels.unit.description: Focus on isolated logic within Abstractions or Core. We minimize mocks and prefer real data structures.
testing.levels.integration.name: Integration Tests
testing.levels.integration.description: Verify the interaction between components, including filesystem IO, SQLite persistence, and plugin loading. These use real Host instances.
testing.levels.e2e.name: End-to-End (E2E) Tests
testing.levels.e2e.description: Validate the entire system from the user's perspective using Playwright to drive the Web console.
testing.entrypoints.title: Test Entrypoints
testing.entrypoints.description: There are two main C# test projects in the `tests/` directory.
testing.entrypoints.table.hostDesc: Verifies HTTP API, gRPC endpoints, and Web console asset mapping. Uses WebApplicationFactory.
testing.entrypoints.table.syncDesc: Focuses on the sync engine, unified node conformance matrix, and deletion policy guards.
testing.matrix.title: Unified Conformance Matrix
testing.matrix.description: All node providers (Local, OneDrive, etc.) must pass a unified conformance matrix. This ensures consistent behavior for capability-driven operations like identity-based deletion and scope boundary resolution.
testing.config.title: Test Configuration
testing.config.description: Tests use YAML-based configuration just like the production service, but with isolated temporary roots.
testing.config.templates.name: Config Templates
testing.config.local.name: Local Overrides
testing.onedrive.title: OneDrive Testing Lane
testing.onedrive.description: OneDrive tests are split into lanes to balance coverage and convenience.
testing.onedrive.offline.name: Offline
testing.onedrive.offline.description: Simulates OneDrive behavior without network calls. Always runs in CI.
testing.onedrive.warmAuth.name: Online (Warm Auth)
testing.onedrive.warmAuth.description: Uses existing refresh tokens in `tests/Config/Local/`. Runs if credentials present.
testing.onedrive.coldAuth.name: Online (Cold Auth)
testing.onedrive.coldAuth.description: Requires interactive login. Used for initial setup or credential refreshing.
testing.onedrive.negative.name: Auth Negative
testing.onedrive.negative.description: Verifies system behavior when authentication fails or tokens expire.
testing.playwright.title: Playwright Browser Tests
testing.playwright.description: E2E tests ensure the Web console works correctly with the backend.
testing.playwright.isolationDesc: Tests run against an isolated Host environment managed by `run-playwright-host.ps1`, ensuring no interference with your development setup.
testing.whenToRun.title: When to Run Which Tests
testing.whenToRun.description: Follow these guidelines to maintain high code quality without slowing down development.
testing.whenToRun.ci.name: PR / CI
testing.whenToRun.local.name: Local Development
testing.whenToRun.local.description: Run relevant integration tests for the feature you are working on.
testing.whenToRun.release.name: Pre-release
testing.whenToRun.release.description: Run the full suite including Online Warm Auth and Playwright E2E.
testing.notes.title: Notes
testing.notes.generated: This document is generated from docs/i18n/targets/testing/template.md and locale resources.
